channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: The Israeli IBD Research Nucleus: collaborative advancements in Israeli IBD research.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Review - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are increasing worldwide, requiring multidisciplinary care and coordinated research infrastructures. Leveraging Israel's integrated healthcare system with IBD cent...

item.title: Human gut flagellome profiling using FlaPro reveals TLR5-related phenotype-specific alterations in IBD.
item.description: Gut microbes - Flagellin, the structural protein of bacterial flagella, activates the innate immune receptor Toll-like receptor 5 (TLR5). However, the ability of different flagellins to bind and stimulate TLR5 va...

item.title: Reconstructing the intestinal microbiota ecological network based on graph neural network and contrastive learning: Predicting key microbiota regulatory targets in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Computational biology and chemistry - Crohn's disease (CD) is a heterogeneous inflammatory bowel disease in which microbial, metabolic, and host factors interact dynamically. Therefore, we aimed to (i) integrate microbiota, metabolite,...

item.title: A scoping review on emerging biomarkers in inflammatory bowel disease: Towards precision medicine in diagnosis and therapeutic management.
item.description: PloS one - Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD) are chronic conditions presenting significant diagnostic and management challenges. Current invasive methods and traditional biomarkers often lack sufficient accur...

item.title: Current and novel biomarkers for predicting and assessing therapeutic response in inflammatory bowel disease: a systematic review.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Review - Despite the availability of various advanced treatments for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), it has been impossible to predict which therapy would offer the best response to the patient. The aim o...

item.title: Quantitative Analysis of Retinal and Choroidal Microvascular Changes in Adult IBD Patients: Insights into the Gut-Retina Axis.
item.description: Ocular immunology and inflammation - To quantitatively evaluate retinal and choroidal microvascular alterations in patients with inflammatory bowel disease using optical coherence tomography angiography and to investigate the potentia...

item.title: From copper imbalance to immunometabolic remodeling: cuproptosis-related vulnerability and therapeutic hypotheses in autoimmune diseases.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Review - Autoimmune diseases (AIDs) are characterized by disrupted immune tolerance, chronic inflammation, and organ-specific tissue injury. Their pathogenesis involves the interplay between genetic suscept...

item.title: Real-World Safety and Herpes Zoster Outcomes After Recombinant Zoster Vaccination in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background/Objectives: Patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are at an increased risk of herpes zoster (HZ), particularly during immunosuppressive treatment. To assess the safety of the re...

item.title: Autoimmune diseases in the era of COVID-19: emerging mechanisms, clinical implications, vaccine considerations, and future directions.
item.description: The Korean journal of internal medicine - Review - The coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic has highlighted a complex, bidirectional relationship between SARSCoV-2 infection and autoimmune diseases. This review examines how SARS-CoV-2 infec...

item.title: Characteristics and outcomes of small-bowel vascular lesions and nonvascular lesions in patients with suspected small-bowel bleeding detected by deep enteroscopy: a single-center experience in Taiwan.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Small-bowel vascular lesions are a major cause of suspected small-bowel bleeding (SSBB), which poses considerable challenges in clinical practice. Hence, this study aimed to identify the clinical c...

item.title: Clinical indications and outcomes of pediatric gastrointestinal endoscopy in a resource-limited healthcare system: Findings from the first multicenter study.
item.description: SAGE open medicine - The objective of this study was to provide the first multicenter evaluation of pediatric gastrointestinal endoscopy in Palestine, specifically characterizing clinical indications, sedation practice...

item.title: Vitamin D3 facilitates mucosal healing and modulates macrophage polarization in DSS-induced colitis, accompanied by restoration of epithelial Hippo-YAP signaling.
item.description: European journal of pharmacology - Damage to the epithelial barrier and dysregulated immune responses are core features of ulcerative colitis (UC). Although vitamin D3 (VD3) is widely recognized for immunomodulatory actions, how it ...

item.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease Is Associated With an Increased Risk of End-Stage Renal Disease: A Population-Based Cohort Study.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is associated with renal complications, but population-based data on end-stage renal disease (ESRD) are limited. To evaluate the incidence of ESRD and renal transpl...

item.title: Selective COX-2 inhibitors for short-term musculoskeletal pain in inflammatory bowel disease in remission: a narrative review.
item.description: BMJ open gastroenterology - Review - Selective cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibitors are often considered when anti-inflammatory analgesia is needed for musculoskeletal pain in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but concern...

item.title: Nutrition and Body Composition: Missing Outcomes in Appetite- and Weight-Modifying Therapies for Individuals with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Gastroenterology -

item.title: Epidemiology of primary sclerosing cholangitis in general and IBD populations: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Hepatology international - Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic cholestatic liver condition that is closely associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). PSC is associated with substantial long-term hepatobi...

item.title: Short, not skinny: The distinct sarcopenia phenotype of pediatric primary sclerosing cholangitis-inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Sarcopenia is prevalent in pediatric liver disease and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and predicts adverse outcomes. We aimed to compare sarcopenia prevalence in pediatric primary sclerosing chol...

item.title: Primary sclerosing cholangitis displays distinct colonic mucosa topography yet a shared mast cell state with ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Nature communications - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic, progressing cholestatic disease that often co-occurs with inflammatory bowel disease (PSC-IBD). PSC-IBD affecting the colon (PSC-ulcerative coliti...

item.title: Integrative Analysis of Genetic and Cellular Interactions Connecting Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Primary Biliary Cholangitis.
item.description: Clinics and research in hepatology and gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) have been reported to coexist in some patients, but the extent of this association remains to be further clarified. Clarifying...

item.title: Multi-omics Mendelian randomization and experimental validation identify PRKAB1 as a regulator of phosphatidylcholine metabolism in IBD.
item.description: Naunyn-Schmiedeberg's archives of pharmacology - Phosphatidylcholines (PCs) are implicated in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), yet the genetic and pathophysiological mechanisms underlying phosphatidylcholine metabolism in IBD remain poorly chara...
